segments: Merchandise - Agriculture, forest and consumer products

supply_chain.dependencies: Dependent on certain key suppliers and manufacturers of locomotive and railroad items due to high barriers of entry and industry-specific requirements
risks: Disruption to key suppliers or manufacturers could negatively impact operating efficiency and increase costs
Competition with other industries for available capacity and raw materials
Limited supplier markets could result in significantly increased prices or material shortages
Constraints on supply chain or operations of connecting carriers may adversely affect operations

regulatory_risks: STB jurisdiction over rates, routes, customer access provisions, fuel surcharges, conditions of service, and extension or abandonment of rail lines
Efforts to increase federal economic regulation of the rail industry
DOT and FRA regulation of safety-related operations
DHS and TSA regulation of security and cybersecurity
Environmental laws and regulations (EPA, state agencies) concerning emissions, discharges, hazardous materials handling and cleanup
CERCLA joint and several liability for cleanup costs at contaminated sites
Federal and state environmental compliance requirements
Potential new or additional laws, regulations, or Executive Orders resulting from the Eastern Ohio Incident
Requirements imposed by government in resolution of government actions, including restrictions on methods of operations
Changes in tax laws and policies
Trade policies including tariffs, border taxes, customs duties, and renegotiation of trade agreements
Railway Labor Act (RLA) governing collective bargaining procedures
FELA (Federal Employer's Liability Act) governing employee personal injury claims
Privacy-related and data protection laws and regulations

sanction_risks: U.S. international trade relationships may adversely impact customers and business
Imposition of new tariffs or adjustments to existing tariffs
Actions by other countries in response to U.S. governmental actions
